headline: John Candy Doc Sparks Emmy Buzz with Reynolds, Hanks at Helm
lede: A new documentary about comedy legend John Candy is generating serious awards season momentum, with Ryan Reynolds and Colin Hanks joining forces to illuminate the beloved performer's legacy.
body:
Hollywood is preparing for a nostalgic deep dive into one of Canada's most cherished comic talents with the upcoming documentary "John Candy: I Like Me", a project that promises intimate insights into the late comedian's extraordinary career.

Reynolds, a fellow Canadian comedic icon, brings personal connection to the project. Having grown up watching Candy's groundbreaking work on SCTV, the "Deadpool" star understands the profound cultural impact of the beloved performer who tragically died in 1994 at just 43 years old.

The documentary tracks Candy's remarkable journey from Second City comedy troupe to Hollywood stardom, featuring never-before-seen archival footage and interviews with family, friends, and comedy contemporaries. Hanks, known for his nuanced storytelling approach, appears to provide narrative depth that goes beyond standard biographical treatment.

Early industry buzz suggests the documentary could be a serious contender in this year's Emmy documentary categories, with Reynolds and Hanks' involvement lending significant credibility to the project.

Cinephiles and comedy fans alike are eagerly anticipating a comprehensive exploration of Candy's life, which promises to celebrate a performer who fundamentally shaped North American comedy in the 1980s and early 1990s.
key_facts: Documentary features Ryan Reynolds and Colin Hanks
Covers John Candy's career from SCTV to Hollywood
